article: A regional education board announced a pilot program for digital library access across rural schools. The program will give students a shared catalog of textbooks, short explainers, and exam preparation material. Officials said the first phase will cover 120 schools and will measure usage, reading time, and search behavior before wider rollout. Teachers asked for short summaries of each resource so students can decide what to open during limited computer-lab time.
highlights: A rural school pilot will provide digital library access to 120 schools. The first phase will measure usage and use short summaries to help students choose resources.

article: A city transport department released a new dashboard for bus delays during peak hours. The dashboard combines GPS pings, depot reports, and passenger complaints to show route-level delay clusters. Analysts found that two transfer corridors caused most of the repeated evening delays. The department plans to test schedule changes on those corridors before changing the wider network.
highlights: A transport dashboard links bus GPS data, depot reports, and complaints. Analysts found two transfer corridors driving repeated evening delays.

article: A health information startup introduced a tool that turns long policy updates into short summaries for insurance support teams. The system tags each update by topic, urgency, and affected process. Managers said the summaries are not treated as legal advice, but they help agents identify which policy document needs closer reading. The company will audit summary errors before expanding the tool.
highlights: A startup is summarizing long policy updates for support teams. The summaries help agents triage documents but are not treated as legal advice.

article: A publishing platform tested a new metadata workflow for story discovery. Editors wrote brief descriptions for older stories that had weak tags and inconsistent blurbs. The platform compared manual blurbs with automatically generated summaries for browsing cards. Product managers said the next step is to measure whether compact summaries improve discovery without hiding important context from readers.
highlights: A publishing platform tested compact story metadata for discovery. The next step is measuring whether summaries improve browsing without losing context.
